_about: Amendment manifest for RFC-0034 + ADR-032 (DN-29 §11). Anchor-keyed, single-pass-per-file (tools/dn29_apply.py). Anchors are unique content substrings, never line/offset, so order is irrelevant. APPLIED 2026-06-24 (21 amendments / 13 files) on maintainer ratification of RFC-0034 + ADR-032. status 'applied' makes the tool refuse a re-apply; a fresh dry-run will now fail loudly (anchors consumed) — the never-silent signal that the edits already landed.
_scope: STRINGENT SCOPE (DN-29 Q11 interpreted carefully): the vocabulary reframe touches the LIVING/GOVERNANCE docs (CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING, README, Glossary) where in-place revision is normal, and the CHARTER criteria (SC-3/FR-M3) get attributed in-place amendments. The Accepted RFCs/ADRs (RFC-0001/0002/0005, ADR-010/011/013/016/017) get APPEND-ONLY FOOTNOTES only — their bodies are NOT rewritten (append-only; house rule #3). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: the ~45 development-process / colloquial uses of 'honest' (swarm-review discipline, 'defer honestly', 'made honestly', 'honest open follow-up', etc.) — these are not the guarantee-model term and rewording them would change meaning. The formal VR-5/G2 rules and the guarantee lattice are unchanged; only the model-vocabulary wording moves.
_footnote_text: Reused below verbatim as the append-only footnote on each relaxed Accepted decision.

docs/Mycelium_Project_Foundation.md.note: SC-3 — unconditional 'zero swaps without a certificate' becomes per-mode; the mode stays never-silent (ADR-032 decision 2).
docs/Mycelium_Project_Foundation.md.anchor: - **SC-3 (no silent swaps).** Static + runtime invariant: zero representation swaps occur without producing an inspectable certificate (auditable; cross-cutting **A.1**, **G9**).
docs/Mycelium_Project_Foundation.md.replacement: - **SC-3 (no silent swaps).** Static + runtime invariant: at the active certification mode (`certified`), zero representation swaps occur without producing an inspectable certificate; in **every** mode the mode itself is never silent — each result is mode-tagged and `EXPLAIN`-able (auditable; cross-cutting **A.1**, **G9**; relaxed to per-mode by RFC-0034 + ADR-032).

docs/Mycelium_Project_Foundation.md.note: FR-M3 — 'always emits a certificate' becomes 'at the active mode'; swap stays never-silent (ADR-032 decision 2).
docs/Mycelium_Project_Foundation.md.anchor: - **FR-M3.** Provide an explicit `swap` operation that never runs implicitly and always emits a certificate (cross-cutting **A.1**; **G2**, **G9**).
docs/Mycelium_Project_Foundation.md.replacement: - **FR-M3.** Provide an explicit `swap` operation that never runs implicitly and, at the active mode (`certified`), emits a certificate; the swap is never-silent in every mode (cross-cutting **A.1**; **G2**, **G9**; relaxed to per-mode by RFC-0034 + ADR-032).

CLAUDE.md.note: 'What this repo is' — reposition the north star + reframe honesty→transparency (ADR-032 decisions 3,4).
CLAUDE.md.anchor:
Mycelium is a unified value-semantics substrate (binary/ternary/dense/VSA) with **certified,
never-silent** representation swaps and **honest, per-operation guarantees**.
CLAUDE.md.replacement: Mycelium is a fast, memory-safe, ergonomic multi-paradigm value-semantics language (binary/ternary/dense/VSA) whose **transparent, never-silent** representation swaps and **per-operation provenance/auditability** are baked in as **optional, tunable** capabilities (`fast` default · `certified` on request — RFC-0034/ADR-032).

CLAUDE.md.note: House rule 1 title — honesty rule → transparency rule (mechanism unchanged).
CLAUDE.md.anchor: 1. **The honesty rule.** Every accuracy/guarantee claim is tagged per-model/per-op on the
CLAUDE.md.replacement: 1. **The transparency rule** *(reframed from "the honesty rule" by ADR-032; mechanism unchanged)*. Every accuracy/guarantee claim is tagged per-model/per-op on the

CLAUDE.md.note: House rule 1 body — 'stay honest' → 'stay accurate' (VR-5 mechanism unchanged).
CLAUDE.md.anchor: always flagged). Downgrade to stay honest; never upgrade without a checked basis (VR-5).
CLAUDE.md.replacement: always flagged). Downgrade to stay accurate; never upgrade without a checked basis (VR-5).

CONTRIBUTING.md.note: Section heading — honesty rule → transparency rule.
CONTRIBUTING.md.replacement: ## The transparency rule (non-negotiable) *(reframed from "the honesty rule" by ADR-032; mechanism unchanged)*

CONTRIBUTING.md.note: 'keep a claim honest' → 'keep a claim accurate'.
CONTRIBUTING.md.anchor: New results may *upgrade* a tag; absence of a proof keeps it weaker. Downgrading to keep a claim honest is always acceptable; upgrading without a checked basis is not.
CONTRIBUTING.md.replacement: New results may *upgrade* a tag; absence of a proof keeps it weaker. Downgrading to keep a claim accurate is always acceptable; upgrading without a checked basis is not.

README.md.note: Headline principle — 'Honesty is a typed, monotone property' → 'Transparency …'.
README.md.anchor: - **Honesty is a typed, monotone property.** A guarantee lattice —
README.md.replacement: - **Transparency is a typed, monotone property.** A guarantee lattice —

README.md.anchor: - **Honesty rule.** Guarantee tags are assigned **per model and per operation**, never in
README.md.replacement: - **Transparency rule.** Guarantee tags are assigned **per model and per operation**, never in

docs/Glossary.md.anchor: The **honesty lattice**: every accuracy/guarantee claim is tagged per-model/per-operation on this
docs/Glossary.md.replacement: The **transparency lattice** *(reframed from "honesty lattice" by ADR-032)*: every accuracy/guarantee claim is tagged per-model/per-operation on this

docs/Glossary.md.anchor: - **Declared** — merely asserted; **always flagged**. The honest floor for anything unproven.
docs/Glossary.md.replacement: - **Declared** — merely asserted; **always flagged**. The transparent floor for anything unproven.

docs/Glossary.md.note: The rule gloss — 'downgrade to stay honest' → 'stay accurate'.
docs/Glossary.md.anchor: **The rule:** *downgrade to stay honest; never upgrade without a checked basis* (VR-5). **Defining doc:**
docs/Glossary.md.replacement: **The rule:** *downgrade to stay accurate; never upgrade without a checked basis* (VR-5). **Defining doc:**

docs/Glossary.md.note: Defining-doc pointer — 'the honesty rule' → 'the transparency rule'.
docs/Glossary.md.anchor: RFC-0001 (the lattice + `Meta`); the honesty rule, CLAUDE.md / Project Foundation. *Usage:* "a
docs/Glossary.md.replacement: RFC-0001 (the lattice + `Meta`); the transparency rule, CLAUDE.md / Project Foundation. *Usage:* "a

docs/rfcs/RFC-0001-Core-IR-and-Metadata-.note: Append-only footnote — RFC-0001 §3.4/§4.6 mandates apply at `certified` (ADR-032 decision 6).
docs/rfcs/RFC-0001-Core-IR-and-Metadata-.anchor: - Re-validate §4.7 once ADR-010 is ratified; promote composed-result default from `Declared` to the foundation's actual composition rules.
docs/rfcs/RFC-0001-Core-IR-and-Metadata-.replacement:
- Re-validate §4.7 once ADR-010 is ratified; promote composed-result default from `Declared` to the foundation's actual composition rules.

> **Footnote — tunable certification (RFC-0034 / ADR-032, 2026-06-24; append-only).** The always-on certification / content-hashing / mandatory guarantee-tag-propagation mandates in this document apply **at the active certification mode (`certified`)**; the `fast` (default) and `balanced` relaxations are governed by **RFC-0034**, and the mode itself is **never silent** (G2 — every result is mode-tagged and `EXPLAIN`-able). The mechanisms here are **unchanged**. See **ADR-032**, which supersedes the *unconditional* reading.

docs/rfcs/RFC-0002-Swap-Certificate-and-.note: Append-only footnote — RFC-0002 §2 per-swap certificate applies at `certified` (ADR-032 decision 6).
docs/rfcs/RFC-0002-Swap-Certificate-and-.anchor: Mechanically checking the *derivations* of lossy-swap bounds end-to-end (vs. axiomatizing cited theorems) is future work; the accepted path axiomatizes theorem statements (tag `Proven`, proof = citation) and checks only the arithmetic instantiation (per ADR-010).
docs/rfcs/RFC-0002-Swap-Certificate-and-.replacement:
Mechanically checking the *derivations* of lossy-swap bounds end-to-end (vs. axiomatizing cited theorems) is future work; the accepted path axiomatizes theorem statements (tag `Proven`, proof = citation) and checks only the arithmetic instantiation (per ADR-010).

> **Footnote — tunable certification (RFC-0034 / ADR-032, 2026-06-24; append-only).** The per-swap certificate emission/checking mandated here applies **at the active certification mode (`certified`)**; the `fast` (default) and `balanced` relaxations are governed by **RFC-0034**, and the swap stays **never silent** in every mode (G2 — mode-tagged + `EXPLAIN`-able). The certificate machinery is **unchanged**. See **ADR-032**, which supersedes the *unconditional* reading.

docs/rfcs/RFC-0005-Selection-Policy-Lang.note: Append-only footnote — RFC-0005 §2 mandatory EXPLAIN; EXPLAIN-of-the-mode stays mandatory (ADR-032 decision 6).
docs/rfcs/RFC-0005-Selection-Policy-Lang.anchor: Provides `SelectionPolicy`/`PolicyRef` for RFC-0001's `Swap` and `Meta.policy_used`; same mechanism serves **RFC-0004** packing schedules; policies content-addressed per RFC-0001 §4.6.
docs/rfcs/RFC-0005-Selection-Policy-Lang.replacement:
Provides `SelectionPolicy`/`PolicyRef` for RFC-0001's `Swap` and `Meta.policy_used`; same mechanism serves **RFC-0004** packing schedules; policies content-addressed per RFC-0001 §4.6.

> **Footnote — tunable certification (RFC-0034 / ADR-032, 2026-06-24; append-only).** Selection/cert machinery is gated by the active certification mode per **RFC-0034**; **`EXPLAIN` of the active mode itself stays mandatory in every mode** (G2 — never silent). The selection mechanism is **unchanged**. See **ADR-032**, which supersedes the *unconditional* reading.

docs/adr/ADR-010-Verified-Numerics-Found.note: Append-only footnote — ADR-010 bound kernels invoked at `certified` (ADR-032 decision 6).
docs/adr/ADR-010-Verified-Numerics-Found.anchor: T0.1 (a–d), T0.2; survey Area 5; RFC-0001 §4.7 / guarantee lattice.
docs/adr/ADR-010-Verified-Numerics-Found.replacement:
T0.1 (a–d), T0.2; survey Area 5; RFC-0001 §4.7 / guarantee lattice.

> **Footnote — tunable certification (RFC-0034 / ADR-032, 2026-06-24; append-only).** The bound kernels / certificate-checking here are invoked **at the active certification mode (`certified`)**; the `fast` (default) and `balanced` relaxations are governed by **RFC-0034** (the mode is never silent — G2). The kernels are **unchanged**. See **ADR-032**, which supersedes the *unconditional* reading.

docs/adr/ADR-011-BoundBasis-Is-Universal.note: Append-only footnote — ADR-011 universal BoundBasis within `certified` (ADR-032 decision 6).
docs/adr/ADR-011-BoundBasis-Is-Universal.anchor: VR-5; survey **G5**. Raised as OQ-3 during M-010 (#5).
docs/adr/ADR-011-BoundBasis-Is-Universal.replacement:
VR-5; survey **G5**. Raised as OQ-3 during M-010 (#5).

> **Footnote — tunable certification (RFC-0034 / ADR-032, 2026-06-24; append-only).** The universal `BoundBasis` applies **within the active certification mode (`certified`)**; the `fast` (default) and `balanced` relaxations are governed by **RFC-0034** (the mode is never silent — G2). The basis machinery is **unchanged**. See **ADR-032**, which supersedes the *unconditional* reading.

docs/adr/ADR-013-Spore-Is-The-Deployable.note: Append-only footnote — ADR-013 spore identity is a COMPILE/DEPLOY hash, available even cert-off (RFC-0034 §8).
docs/adr/ADR-013-Spore-Is-The-Deployable.replacement:
amended to r2 (scope note only).

> **Footnote — tunable certification (RFC-0034 / ADR-032, 2026-06-24; append-only).** Spore content-hash identity is a **compile/deploy-phase** concern and **remains available even when the runtime certification mode is fully off** (`fast`) — deployability survives a cert-off runtime (RFC-0034 §8). Disabling the *compile* spore hash is a separate, explicit, `EXPLAIN`-ed choice (never silent — G2). The identity mechanism is **unchanged**. See **ADR-032**.

docs/adr/ADR-016-Interpreted-Compiled-AB.note: Append-only footnote — ADR-016 ABI dispatch keys are compile-time hashes (RFC-0034 §8).
docs/adr/ADR-016-Interpreted-Compiled-AB.replacement:
honest deferral, VR-5). Append-only.

> **Footnote — tunable certification (RFC-0034 / ADR-032, 2026-06-24; append-only).** The ABI dispatch keys are **compile-time** content hashes and **remain available with the runtime certification mode off** (`fast`) — RFC-0034 §8. The ABI mechanism is **unchanged**. See **ADR-032**.

docs/adr/ADR-017-Hot-Inject-Recompiled-D.note: Append-only footnote — ADR-017 hot-inject dispatch keys are compile-time hashes (RFC-0034 §8).
docs/adr/ADR-017-Hot-Inject-Recompiled-D.replacement:
(MLIR→LLVM; §10 OQ-3) — honest scope, VR-5. Append-only.

> **Footnote — tunable certification (RFC-0034 / ADR-032, 2026-06-24; append-only).** The hot-inject dispatch keys are **compile-time** content hashes and **remain available with the runtime certification mode off** (`fast`) — RFC-0034 §8. The inject mechanism is **unchanged**. See **ADR-032**.
